content.full_text:
April 23, 1976


Millard Davidson
Executive Director
Republican Associates
1153 North Brand Boulevard
Glendale CA 91202

Dear Mr. Davidson:

     Please excuse my delay in responding to our telephone con-
versation and your subsequent letter of March 17, 1976.

     You asked whether any of the costs of producing the Republican
Associates Newsletter could be considered to be "expenditures." The
newsletter is published six to eight times a year and is distributed
to approximately 1,500 members of Republican Associates and other
Republicans.

     During the last few weeks, the Commission has considered regu-
lations defining the meaning of "contribution" and "expenditure" as
used in the Political Reform Act. Consideration of these regulations
involved a determination of whether or not the costs incurred in
printing and distributing a newsletter are "expenditures." In these
regulations adopted April 22, 1976, the Commission determined that
the costs of publishing a regularly published newsletter which is
distributed to members or other affiliated persons are not expendi-
tures. The Republican Associates Newsletter would appear to
fall within this exemption and, thus, would not be subject to any
of the disclosure or reporting requirements imposed by the Act.
If, however, you incur additional publishing costs by printing
campaign materials, these additional costs would be expenditures.
A copy of the regulations is enclosed.

     I hope this information will be of assistance. Please contact
me if you have any additional questions.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Natalie E. West
                                        Staff Counsel
NEW:PVP
cc:  William McCormick
     Republican Central Committee
     of Los Angeles County
     619 South New Hampshire
     Los Angeles CA 90005
